clean_text:
CENTRAL RESERVE POLICE FORCE (CRPF)
DIRECTORATE GENERAL   ENGINEERING WING
CGO Complex, Lodhi Road, New Delhi   110003
NOTICE INVITING TENDER (NIT)
Tender Reference No.: CRPF/EW/2025-26/BOP-RAJ/0042
Subject: CONSTRUCTION OF 06 (SIX) BORDER OUTPOST BUILDINGS IN RAJASTHAN SECTOR
         (BARMER AND JAISALMER DISTRICTS)
GeM Bid Number: GEM/2025/B/5234871
Published on: Central Public Procurement Portal (CPPP) & GeM
Date of Publication: 15th April 2025
Last Date for Submission of Bids: 20th May 2025 (1400 hrs IST)
Date of Opening of Technical Bids: 21st May 2025 (1100 hrs IST)
Estimated Cost: Rs. 18,50,00,000/- (Rupees Eighteen Crore Fifty Lakh Only)
Earnest Money Deposit (EMD): Rs. 37,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Seven Lakh Only)
Performance Security: 3% of contract value as ePBG
SECTION 1: INTRODUCTION AND SCOPE OF WORK
1.1  The Director General, CRPF, invites sealed tenders (Two-Bid System
     Technical Bid and Financial Bid) from eligible contractors for the
     construction of 06 (six) Border Outpost (BOP) buildings in Rajasthan
     Sector, as per the specifications and terms laid down in this document.
1.2  The work involves:
     (a) Construction of 06 BOP buildings (each approx. 450 sqm built-up area)
     (b) Internal electrification and plumbing
     (c) Solar power installation (5 KW per BOP)
     (d) Water harvesting structure at each location
     (e) Perimeter fencing (chain-link, 8 ft height)
     (f) Approach road construction (WBM + BT surface, 200m per BOP)
1.3  Period of Completion: 12 months from the date of issue of Work Order.
1.4  Defect Liability Period: 24 months from the date of completion.
SECTION 2: ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA
Bidders must satisfy ALL of the following eligibility criteria. Failure to
meet any mandatory criterion will result in rejection of the bid.
2.1  FINANCIAL CRITERIA
2.1.1  Annual Turnover: The bidder must have an average annual turnover of
       not less than Rs. 5 Crore (Rupees Five Crore) during the last three
       financial years (FY 2022-23, FY 2023-24, FY 2024-25), as certified
       by a Chartered Accountant.
2.1.2  Net Worth: The bidder must have a positive net worth as on 31st March
       2025, certified by a Chartered Accountant.
2.1.3  Solvency Certificate: The bidder must submit a Solvency Certificate
       from a Scheduled Bank for an amount not less than Rs. 3 Crore.
2.2  EXPERIENCE CRITERIA
2.2.1  Similar Work Experience: The bidder must have successfully completed
       at least TWO (2) similar works (construction of government/defense
       buildings) of value not less than Rs. 7 Crore each, OR ONE (1) similar
       work of value not less than Rs. 12 Crore, during the last SEVEN (7)
       years ending 31st March 2025.
2.2.2  Government Contract Experience: The bidder must have experience of
       executing at least one contract with a Central/State Government
       department or Public Sector Undertaking (PSU).
2.3  TECHNICAL CRITERIA
2.3.1  Registration: The bidder must be registered as a contractor with
       CPWD / MES / State PWD / Railways in Class-I or equivalent category.
2.3.2  ISO Certification: The bidder should possess a valid ISO 9001:2015
       Quality Management System certification.
2.3.3  Labour License: The bidder must hold a valid Contract Labour License
       under the Contract Labour (Regulation and Abolition) Act, 1970.
2.4  COMPLIANCE CRITERIA
2.4.1  GST Registration: The bidder must have a valid GST Registration
       (GSTIN) and must be compliant with GST return filing.
2.4.2  PAN Card: The bidder must submit a copy of the PAN Card.
2.4.3  No Blacklisting: The bidder must submit a self-declaration that the
       firm has not been blacklisted or debarred by any Central/State
       Government department or PSU as on the date of bid submission.
2.4.4  EMD Submission: The bidder must submit EMD of Rs. 37,00,000/- in the
       form of BG/FDR/DD in favour of "Pay & Accounts Officer, CRPF".
       Note: MSEs registered under MSMED Act and Startups recognized by
       DPIIT are EXEMPT from EMD as per GFR 2017 Rule 170(i).
SECTION 3: EVALUATION METHODOLOGY
3.1  Evaluation shall be done in Two-Bid System:
     (a) Technical Bid: Evaluated first. Only technically qualified bidders
         will have their Financial Bids opened.
     (b) Financial Bid: L1 (Lowest Bidder) methodology.
3.2  Technical Evaluation will be on Pass/Fail basis against the eligibility
     criteria listed in Section 2.
3.3  The Evaluation Committee reserves the right to seek clarification from
     any bidder regarding their submission.
3.4  Bidders scoring below the technical threshold will NOT have their
     financial bids opened.
SECTION 4: BID SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS
4.1  Technical Bid Envelope shall contain:
     (a) Covering letter on company letterhead
     (b) EMD proof (BG/FDR/DD) or MSE/Startup exemption certificate
     (c) Audited balance sheets for last 3 financial years
     (d) CA certificate for average annual turnover
     (e) Work completion certificates for similar works
     (f) CPWD/MES/PWD registration certificate
     (g) ISO 9001:2015 certificate
     (h) Contract Labour License
     (i) GST Registration Certificate
     (j) PAN Card copy
     (k) Self-declaration regarding non-blacklisting
     (l) Company incorporation certificate
     (m) Board resolution / Power of Attorney
4.2  Financial Bid: To be submitted ONLY in the prescribed BOQ format
     (attached separately as BOQ_Price_Schedule.csv).
SECTION 5: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS
5.1  The Competent Authority reserves the right to reject any or all bids
     without assigning any reason thereof.
5.2  Conditional bids shall be summarily rejected.
5.3  Joint ventures / consortia are NOT permitted for this tender.
5.4  Sub-contracting of more than 25% of the work value is not permitted
     without prior written approval of the Competent Authority.
5.5  All disputes shall be subject to the jurisdiction of courts in New Delhi.
Date: 15th April 2025
DISCLAIMER: This is a synthetic document created for software testing purposes.
No government endorsement claimed or implied.
ADDITIONAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS (ATC)
Tender Ref: CRPF/EW/2025-26/BOP-RAJ/0042
NOTE: These Additional Terms and Conditions are SUPPLEMENTARY to the
General Terms and Conditions (GTC) of GeM and the terms in the RFP.
WHERE THESE ATCs CONFLICT WITH THE STANDARD GTC, THESE ATCs SHALL PREVAIL.
ATC-1: GEOGRAPHICAL EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENT (ADDITIONAL)
In addition to the experience criteria stated in the RFP (Section 2.2),
the bidder MUST demonstrate experience of executing at least ONE civil
construction project in a desert/arid region (Rajasthan, Gujarat-Kutch,
or equivalent terrain) within the last 5 years.
This is an ADDITIONAL mandatory requirement not covered in the standard GTC.
ATC-2: SITE VISIT MANDATORY
Bidders are required to have visited at least ONE of the proposed BOP sites
before bid submission. A Site Visit Certificate signed by the CRPF Station
Commander at the respective location must be submitted with the Technical Bid.
Site visit window: 20th April 2025 to 5th May 2025.
Contact for site visit arrangement: SE (Border Works), CRPF Jodhpur Sector.
Phone: 0291-XXXXXXX
ATC-3: SECURITY CLEARANCE
The successful bidder's key personnel (Project Manager, Site Engineer) will
be subject to security verification by CRPF Intelligence Wing before being
permitted access to the border area construction sites.
Personnel who fail security clearance must be replaced within 15 days,
failing which a penalty of Rs. 50,000 per day of delay shall be levied.
ATC-4: MATERIAL PROCUREMENT
(a) All cement used shall be OPC 53 Grade conforming to IS 12269:2013.
    Only cement from approved manufacturers (ACC, Ambuja, UltraTech,
    Shree Cement, JK Cement) shall be used.
(b) Steel reinforcement shall conform to IS 1786:2008, Fe-500D grade.
    Mill test certificates must be submitted for every lot.
(c) Water for construction must be sourced locally. If potable water is
    not available, the contractor must arrange water tankers at own cost.
    Water testing report from a NABL-accredited lab required before use.
ATC-5: MILESTONE PAYMENT SCHEDULE
Payment shall be released in the following milestones:
  Milestone 1: Foundation complete (30% of contract value)
  Milestone 2: Superstructure complete (25% of contract value)
  Milestone 3: Finishing and MEP works (25% of contract value)
  Milestone 4: Final completion + defect rectification (15% of contract value)
  Retention: 5% retained for 24 months as defect liability period
Note: GST shall be paid extra at applicable rates on each milestone payment.
ATC-6: LIQUIDATED DAMAGES
In case of delay beyond the stipulated completion period of 12 months:
  - Penalty: 0.5% of contract value per week of delay
  - Maximum penalty: 10% of total contract value
  - Beyond 10% penalty: CRPF reserves the right to terminate the contract
    and forfeit the Performance Security
ATC-7: REVISED TURNOVER REQUIREMENT
IMPORTANT AMENDMENT: The minimum average annual turnover requirement specified
in the RFP (Section 2.1.1) is hereby REVISED from Rs. 5 Crore to Rs. 4 Crore
(Rupees Four Crore Only) to enable wider participation from competent firms.
This revision supersedes the original RFP clause. All other financial criteria
remain unchanged.
Date: 18th April 2025
DISCLAIMER: Synthetic document for software testing. No government endorsement.
CORRIGENDUM NO. 1
Tender Reference: CRPF/EW/2025-26/BOP-RAJ/0042
GeM Bid Number: GEM/2025/B/5234871
Date: 02nd May 2025
The following amendments are hereby made to the original NIT/RFP document
dated 15th April 2025 and all associated documents:
AMENDMENT 1: EXTENSION OF BID SUBMISSION DEADLINE
The last date for submission of bids is hereby EXTENDED as follows:
  Original Date: 20th May 2025, 1400 hrs IST
  Revised Date:  30th May 2025, 1400 hrs IST
  Original Date of Opening: 21st May 2025
  Revised Date of Opening:  31st May 2025, 1100 hrs IST
AMENDMENT 2: EXPERIENCE CRITERIA CLARIFICATION
Clause 2.2.1 of the RFP is clarified as follows:
"Similar Work" shall mean construction of residential/office/institutional
buildings for Central Government, State Government, Defence establishments,
or Central/State PSUs. Construction of commercial buildings, private
residences, or industrial sheds shall NOT be considered as "similar work."
Work completion certificates must clearly mention:
  (a) Nature of work executed
  (b) Gross value of the contract
  (c) Date of completion
  (d) Name and designation of the issuing officer
AMENDMENT 3: EMD FORMAT
In addition to BG/FDR/DD, the EMD may also be submitted as:
  - Online transfer (NEFT/RTGS) to CRPF account
  - Bid Security Declaration (as per DoE OM dated 12.11.2020)
MSE and DPIIT-recognized Startup exemptions continue to apply as per
GFR 2017 Rule 170(i) and Rule 173(i) respectively.
AMENDMENT 4: SOLAR POWER SPECIFICATION UPGRADE
The solar power system requirement in Clause 1.2(c) of the RFP is revised:
  Original: 5 KW per BOP
  Revised:  7.5 KW per BOP (to accommodate increased power requirements
            for surveillance equipment)
The BOQ shall be deemed amended accordingly. Bidders should price for
7.5 KW solar systems in their financial bids.
All other terms and conditions of the original NIT/RFP and ATC remain
unchanged unless specifically amended herein.
This Corrigendum is to be read in conjunction with the original tender
documents. In case of conflict between this Corrigendum and the original
documents, this Corrigendum shall prevail.
DISCLAIMER: Synthetic document for software testing. No government endorsement.
